raw_consumer_statement: I am a XXXX XXXX XXXX service member. On XX/XX/XXXX I left the United States for the XXXX XXXX. On XX/XX/XXXX in the middle of the night I noticed that someone from XXXX XXXX logged into my account on XXXX  On this XXXX account my American Express credit card was saved. After noticing this unusual activity I quickly reached out to XXXX to change my password. During this time the fraudster started making ticket purchases and began trying to transfer these tickets to themselves. I noticed this a froze my American Express card and reported the fraud to American Express. Over the next few days I worked with XXXX, who refused to assist me and asked that any claim be processed with the credit card company. American Express refunded my account but said that a full review could reverse that decision. Now yesterday, American Express emailed me claiming that the fraudulent charges are rejected by the merchant and they will now debit me for the full amount. I asked for the merchants proof, and provided them a copy of my military orders to the XXXX XXXX, emails showing that someone logged into my account fraudulently, as well as the proof that multiple further purchases were attempted against my card while my card was frozen.
